etymology.description: 一(一) (yī, "one") + 生(生) (shēng, "life") = all one's life or a lifetime, referring to the entirety of a person's life.
etymology.interpretation: The word 一生 (yìshēng) conveys the concept of a singular, continuous span of life. By combining 一(一) (yī, "one") and 生(生) (shēng, "life"), it symbolizes the whole duration from birth to death as a single unit.

examples.chinese: 他一生都在追求艺术的完美(他一生都在追求藝術的完美)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yìshēng dōu zài zhuīqiú yìshù de wánměi.
examples.english: He spent his entire life pursuing artistic perfection.

examples.chinese: 小明希望能一生幸福(小明希望能一生幸福)。
examples.english: Xiaoming hopes to have a lifetime of happiness.

examples.chinese: 她的一生充满了冒险和挑战(她的一生充滿了冒險和挑戰)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yìshēng chōngmǎnle màoxiǎn hé tiǎozhàn.
examples.english: Her life was full of adventures and challenges.
